name: Saucy Swedish Meatballs
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Fresh green beans, cut into
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the egg, bread crumbs, club soda, 3 tbsp chopped dill, 3/4 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp pepper. Using your hands or a wooden spoon, blend well.
2. Shape the mixture into 1 1/4 inch balls.
3. In a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat, melt 3 tbsp butter. Add the meatballs; cook about 12 minutes, turning frequently until browned on all sides.
4. Meanwhile, prepare noodles according to package directions and add green beans to boiling water along with noodles (do not add salt).
5. When the meatballs are browned, remove them to a plate using a slotted spoon.
6. To the drippings in the skillet, add the remaining 1 tbsp butter; melt over medium-high heat.
7. Add mushrooms and cook about 5 minutes, stirring frequently until crisp-tender.
8. Return the meatballs to the skillet along with 3/4 cup water, the remaining 3 tbsp chopped dill, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp pepper. Increase heat to high; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low and cook, covered, for 5 minutes until the meatballs are cooked through.
9. Stir in sour cream and cook an additional 2 to 3 minutes until heated through.
10. To serve: Drain noodles and green beans. Arrange on a serving platter. Spoon the meatball mixture over the noodles; toss quickly. Garnish with dill sprigs if desired. Makes 4 servings.
